title: Lung Mass - Lobectomy for NSCLC
chief_complaint: 60-year-old heavy smoker (40 pack-years) with 3cm solitary right upper lobe mass on screening CT. PET-CT positive, no mediastinal involvement. Biopsy: adenocarcinoma. PFT adequate for resection.

hidden_data.pet_ct: SUVmax 8.5, no mediastinal or distant uptake
hidden_data.pft: FEV1: 2.2L (78% predicted), DLCO: 72%, predicted post-op FEV1: 1.5L (>40%)
hidden_data.biopsy: Invasive adenocarcinoma, EGFR wild-type, ALK negative

correct_diagnosis: Stage IA Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (T1bN0M0) - Surgical Candidate
key_learning_points: Lobectomy = standard for stage I NSCLC
Adjuvant chemotherapy if stage II+ or high risk

patient_responses.symptoms: No cough or chest pain, found on screening

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Stage IA NSCLC - excellent candidate for lobectomy.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is stage I NSCLC. Lobectomy is standard of care.
feedback.attending_note: NSCLC Stage IA: VATS right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node sampling. 5-year survival >80% for stage I. Smoking cessation essential. Annual surveillance CT for 5 years.
